channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: In their own words: What Trump said about Gaza and how top administration officials contradicted him
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Top officials in President Donald Trump's administration have contradicted some of his recent comments about the United States taking long-term control of Gaza. They also have played down the possibility of sending in U.S.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 2nd federal judge in 2 days blocks Trump's birthright citizenship order
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — A second federal judge in two days has blocked President Donald Trump’s effort to end birthright citizenship for the children of parents who are in the U.S. illegally, decrying what he described as the administration's attempt to ignore the Constitution for political gain. U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON and MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Superintendent forum interrupted by pornographic image
item.description: An online superintendent forum at noon Tuesday was interrupted by a pornographic image, according to a spokesperson with the Liberty County School System.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Smith, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bond set at $20M for man accused in shooting at Ohio warehouse that killed 2 and wounded 4
item.description: NEW ALBANY, Ohio (AP) — A warehouse worker said in a frantic 911 call that he was shot in the neck and then took the gun from a co-worker after he had shot several others. Two people died and four were injured in the Ohio shooting that sent panicked employees fleeing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Sports reporter dies while in New Orleans for Super Bowl LIX
item.description: Adan Manzano died on Wednesday in New Orleans while on assignment to cover the Super Bowl for both Telemundo Kansas City and Tico Sports.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Mueller, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cost to sequester Megan Boswell jury thousands per day
item.description: "That's one way they can serve their country is to take their jury duty," explained Circuit Court Clerk Bobby Russell. "And that is a very important service.
item.dc:creator: Karen Jenkins, NewsNation

item.title: DOGE officials home in on Medicare, Medicaid offices
item.description: It was unclear which systems DOGE had access to and whether any sensitive medical information was part of the efforts.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Federal judge pauses Trump federal worker buyouts
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday extended the deadline for federal workers to accept the Trump administration’s sweeping buyout offer. The court will weigh the merits of the bid to block the offer in a Monday hearing.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's Gaza plan shocks the world but finds support in Israel
item.description: President Donald Trump’s plan to seek U.S. ownership of the Gaza Strip and move out its population has found a sympathetic ear in Israel.
item.dc:creator: Tia Goldenberg, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Justice Sotomayor renews her opposition to the court's ruling that ex-presidents have broad immunity
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — U.S. Supreme Court Justice Sonia Sotomayor said her conservative colleagues are risking the court's legitmacy with decisions affording President Donald Trump broad immunity and overturning longstanding precedents on other issues.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fentanyl with $3M estimated street value seized; Man in custody
item.description: Deputies arrested a North Carolina man after seizing nearly 7 pounds of fentanyl with a street value of $3 million.
item.dc:creator: Dennis Bright, NewsNation

item.title: Second type of bird flu found in US dairy cows
item.description: Experts said it raises new questions about wider spread and the difficulty of controlling infections in animals and the people who work closely with them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump blames 'obsolete' US air traffic control system for the plane and chopper collision near DC
item.description: President Donald Trump says he thinks last week’s deadly collision between a passenger jet and Army helicopter wouldn't have happened if the U.S. had a more modern air traffic control computer system.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Freezing hikers stuck in waist-deep snow rescued during Mount Washington whiteout
item.description: The two friends had hiked New Hampshire's challenging Mount Washington trail a month ago. They returned and did it again Sunday after trekking through neighboring peaks in the White Mountains.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 5 things to watch as the federal buyout deadline approaches
item.description: As the deadline approaches, agencies have amped up the pressure on employees, sending frequent “reminders” and a copy of the contract they would have to sign to accept the deferred resignation.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: A dozen West Point cadet clubs ordered to disband, all others paused
item.description: The U.S. Military Academy at West Point, New York, has ordered a dozen cadet extracurricular and social clubs to immediately and permanently "cease all activities," with all affected groups centered on gender, race and ethnicity, the school acknowledged Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Trump doubles down on Gaza Strip idea, says no US soldiers would be needed
item.description: The president in an early Thursday post expanded on his idea, making clear he was serious after some advisers sought to clarify or temper aspects of the proposal.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy shares conditions for ending Ukraine-Russia war and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FAA, NTSB to brief senators on Washington, DC midair collision
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration and National Transportation Safety Board are planning to brief senators just one week after a midair collision claimed the lives of 67 people.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Racial gap widened in deaths among US moms around the time of childbirth
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Black women in the U.S. died at a rate nearly 3.5 times higher than white women around the time of childbirth in 2023, as maternal mortality fell below prepandemic levels overall but racial gaps widened, according to federal health data released Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Texas school district warns parents Border Patrol may question or deport students
item.description: Parents across Texas are being warned that Border Patrol agents may be boarding school buses and their children may be detained or deported if they can not prove citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Casey Anthony and Megan Boswell parallels are 'scary': Lawyer
item.description: "There's a lot of similarities," Casey Anthony's attorney said. "A beautiful baby found dead. The question is who, where, when and why.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Banfield, NewsNation

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks Trump plan offering incentives for federal workers to resign
item.description: A federal judge has blocked President Donald Trump’s plan to push out federal employees by offering them financial incentives.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Collin Binkley And Byron Tau, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Department of Education: The arguments for, against killing it
item.description: Former Trump campaign surrogate Scotti Nell Hughes says the move would bring more accountability at the state level.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: A Texas man is executed for the killing of a pastor during a robbery at a church
item.description: HUNTSVILLE, Texas (AP) — A Texas man convicted of beating and suffocating a Dallas area pastor in his church during a robbery was put to death Wednesday evening, the second execution in the U.S. this year and the first of four scheduled in Texas over the next three months.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO and MICHAEL GRACZYK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Protests erupt around the country against the new Trump administration's policies
item.description: Protesters in cities across the U.S. hit the streets to voice their objection to President Donald Trump's priorities when it comes to immigration reform, gender, and labor.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Japan Airlines plane clips Delta flight at Sea-Tac airport
item.description: A plane at Seattle-Tacoma International Airport hit another aircraft that was taxiing on the runway Wednesday morning, the Federal Aviation Administration has confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Man jailed over police AI program, then freed 17 months after victim raised doubts
item.description: Chris Gatlin spent 17 months in jail for a crime that an artificial intelligence program said he committed, only to be freed after the prosecutor learned there was no real evidence.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rare invasive bug intercepted at Detroit airport
item.description: Agriculture specialists with U.S. Customs and Border Protection confirm that rare invasive seed bugs were intercepted on undeclared cargo late last year at Detroit Metro Airport.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, Fox 5

item.title: Celebrities rail against Trump's policies
item.description: After years of launching blistering attacks on President Donald Trump, much of Hollywood appears to be casting him in a new "Harry Potter"-inspired role: He Who Must Not Be Named.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KRON4

item.title: Armed teen arrested at Arkansas airport after allegedly demanding plane
item.description: After the employees got to safety, the suspect "breached through a door onto the airfield," an airport official said.
item.dc:creator: Hunter Trombetta, KRON4

item.title: US allies, adversaries reject Trump suggestion to 'take over' Gaza Strip
item.description: Both U.S. allies and adversaries alike rejected President Donald Trump’s suggestion that the U.S. “take over” the war-torn Gaza Strip and turn it into the “Riviera of the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Remains of all 67 victims in deadly DC plane and chopper collision now recovered
item.description: The chief medical examiner is still trying to positively identify one set of remains, officials said in a news release.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump picks former chief entangled in 'Sharpiegate' to lead NOAA
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has nominated Neil Jacobs to lead the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, turning to the man who was his acting NOAA chief in 2019 when Trump altered an expected hurricane impact map in what became known as the “Sharpiegate” scandal.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Remains of all 67 victims in deadly DC plane and chopper collision now recovered
item.description: The chief medical examiner is still trying to positively identify one set of remains, officials said in a news release.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
